title: Run Stakes — Pre-Run Intent with Bonus Drop
problem: Every run starts with no declared intention beyond class selection. Players in short sessions (10–15 min) have no micro-goal to anchor the run — only the macro goal of reaching floor 30. Without a session-scoped win condition, a run that ends on floor 4 feels like pure failure even if the player achieved something meaningful.

lineage.spawnReason: scope split: pre-run intent layer not covered by class-system — adds hunt declaration + probability-gated reward

analysis: The session-length problem: a player with 12 minutes has no declared win condition smaller than 'survive to floor 30'. Without a micro-goal, any run ending before that feels like failure. Run Stakes adds a pre-run intent declaration on ClassSelectScreen — the player picks a hunt (SLAYER/SCAVENGER/DEEPDIVER) before descending. On completion, a 35% probability rolls a bonus chest-quality item drop. The stake persists across runs (it's the player's declared style of play), and completion state resets each startRun. The probability gate preserves run variance and avoids the stake feeling like a guaranteed economy loop.
implementation: gameStore.ts: StakeId type ('slayer'|'scavenger'|'deepdiver'), STAKE_DEFS array (exported), activeStake/stakeClaimed/stakeBonusItem/echoWhisperShown added to GameState. claimStake() uses makeRng(seed^0xd4e5f612^floor) to roll 35% — if hit, calls rollLoot('chest', floor, 50) and stores first item as stakeBonusItem. Stake checks wired into recordKill (slayer threshold 25), recordRunItem (scavenger threshold 10), nextFloor (deepdiver threshold absFloor 5). startRun resets stakeClaimed+stakeBonusItem, leaves activeStake unchanged. ClassSelectScreen: stake picker row (3 chips: ⚔ SLAYER / 📦 SCAVENGER / ↓ DEEPDIVER) above DESCEND button; active chip gold-bordered; hint text shows target and '35% bonus drop on success'. GridScreen: top-right progress badge (e.g. SLAYER 18/25) visible while stake active and unclaimed; '★ STAKE CLAIMED' toast on completion (4s); if bonus item present, adds to bag via inventoryStore.addItem and shows '★ STAKE CLAIMED — bonus item added to bag'.

decisions.decision: activeStake persists across runs — not reset by startRun
decisions.rationale: The stake represents the player's declared hunt style, not a per-run commitment. A player who always wants to slay should not have to re-select every run. stakeClaimed resets each run, so the reward mechanic still triggers once per completion.
alternativesConsieded: Reset stake each run — forces re-selection, adds friction for returning players
decisions.recommendation: Persist activeStake, reset only stakeClaimed. Stack-agnostic: both are plain gameStore fields.

decisions.rationale: A guaranteed drop would make stakes feel like a farming mechanic — players would optimise for the fastest completable stake rather than their preferred playstyle. 35% is frequent enough to feel meaningful but rare enough to stay exciting. The RNG is seeded from (seed ^ constant ^ floor) to maintain run reproducibility.
alternativesConsidered: 10% drop — feels unrewarding, players stop caring about claims
decisions.recommendation: 35% seeded roll in claimStake(). Chest-quality loot at current floor depth with 50% magic find bonus.

decisions.decision: Stake bonus item delivered directly to bag — no new loot screen
decisions.rationale: Opening a loot screen mid-run for a single item would break the movement flow and require wiring a new loot entry point. Directly calling inventoryStore.addItem() with a toast notification delivers the reward without interrupting the game loop.
alternativesConsidered: New 'stake loot' screen — higher friction, requires additional state routing
decisions.recommendation: addItem() + GridScreen toast. If bag is full the item is silently dropped — acceptable for a 35% bonus.

successCriteria: Stake picker visible on ClassSelectScreen footer. Progress badge updates in real-time in GridScreen. claimStake fires exactly once per run at the correct threshold for each stake type. Bonus item appears in bag at 35% rate (verifiable over many runs via sim). Toast displays on claim. TypeScript 0 errors.
knownLimitations: DEEPDIVER progress counter shows absoluteFloor (e.g. tier 2 floor 1 = floor 11) rather than a simple 'reached floor 5 in this run' — could confuse returning players on higher tiers
Stake completion not recorded in careerStats (stakesCompleted counter not yet added)
Bag-full edge case silently drops the bonus item — no fallback to stash or notification
userGuide:
## Run Stakes

Before descending, pick a **Hunt** in the footer below the class cards:

- **⚔ SLAYER** — Kill 25 monsters this run
- **📦 SCAVENGER** — Pick up 10 items this run  
- **↓ DEEPDIVER** — Reach floor 5

Your hunt is optional — tap a chip to select, tap again to deselect. Your choice persists between runs.

While a stake is active, a small badge in the top-right of the dungeon shows your progress (e.g. `SLAYER 12/25`). When you hit the target, the badge disappears and a `★ STAKE CLAIMED` toast appears. There's a **35% chance** a bonus item drops into your bag at that moment — chest-quality loot scaled to your current floor depth.
componentFile: src/store/gameStore.ts (StakeId, STAKE_DEFS, claimStake, activeStake, stakeBonusItem), src/screens/ClassSelectScreen.tsx, src/screens/GridScreen.tsx

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial full feature definition — Run Stakes implemented and verified

publicInterface.name: STAKE_DEFS
publicInterface.description: { id, label, desc, icon, target } — exported from gameStore for UI consumption

publicInterface.name: GameState.activeStake
publicInterface.description: Currently selected stake; persists across startRun

publicInterface.name: GameState.stakeClaimed
publicInterface.description: True once the stake objective was met this run

publicInterface.name: GameState.stakeBonusItem
publicInterface.description: Set to a loot item when 35% drop triggers on claim; consumed by GridScreen

publicInterface.name: claimStake
publicInterface.description: Called internally when objective is met; 35% chance to set stakeBonusItem

publicInterface.name: clearStakeBonusItem
publicInterface.description: Called by GridScreen after delivering bonus item to inventory

codeSnippets.label: 35% bonus drop on stake claim
Stake picker UI in ClassSelectScreen
codeSnippets.snippet:
<View style={styles.stakeRow}>
  <Text style={styles.stakeLabel}>HUNT</Text>
  {STAKE_DEFS.map(s => (
    <TouchableOpacity key={s.id}
      style={[styles.stakeChip, activeStake === s.id && styles.stakeChipActive]}
      onPress={() => setStake(activeStake === s.id ? null : s.id)}>
      <Text style={styles.stakeChipIcon}>{s.icon}</Text>
      <Text style={[styles.stakeChipText, activeStake === s.id && styles.stakeChipTextActive]}>{s.label}</Text>
    </TouchableOpacity>
  ))}
</View>

implementationNotes: activeStake persists across startRun intentionally — the player declares a season-long hunt, not a per-run one. Only stakeClaimed and stakeBonusItem reset on startRun.
The seeded RNG for the bonus drop uses seed XOR 0xd4e5f612 XOR (floor * 0x9e3779b9) — XOR-mixed to avoid correlation with the floor's item RNG while still being deterministic per run.

Bonus item delivery is async: claimStake() sets stakeBonusItem in store, then GridScreen's useEffect watches it and calls invStore.addItem() + clearStakeBonusItem(). This avoids direct inventory coupling in gameStore.

Claim trigger sites: recordKill (slayer, kills >= 25), recordRunItem (scavenger, runItemsFound >= 10), nextFloor (deepdiver, absFloor >= 5). All three call claimStake() which guards against double-claim with stakeClaimed.
